page: Vultr voc-c-4c-8gb-75s-amd is an x86_64 compute-optimized server instance featuring 4 vCPUs and 4 physical cores powered by an AMD EPYC Genoa processor running at 2.0 GHz. The hardware configuration includes 8.0 GB of system memory, yielding 2.0 GB of RAM per core, and is bundled with 75 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. The CPU allocation is shared, and the processor features a cache layout of 32 KB L1d, 1.0 MB L2, and 32 MB L3 cache, alongside support for AVX-512 and cryptography extensions like AES and SHA_NI.

Benchmark results indicate top-tier single-core CPU performance, with stress-ng single-core tests placing in the top 10% and single-threaded bzip3 decompression also reaching the top 10%. Multi-core CPU performance and memory bandwidth read operations fall within the average range. However, the shared CPU allocation presents tradeoffs, as multi-threaded bzip3 decompression and database operations perform in the bottom 25%, and static web serving throughput is similarly limited.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, this instance offers a resource-dense compute profile utilizing modern AMD Genoa architecture without the premium of dedicated cores. It is appropriate for workloads such as single-threaded processing tasks, basic web serving, light containerized applications, and utility scripting where high single-core burst capability is required but sustained multi-threaded database operations are not critical.
description: Vultr voc-c-4c-8gb-75s-amd is an x86_64 compute-optimized server instance configured with 4 shared vCPUs and 4 physical cores on an AMD EPYC Genoa processor clocked at 2.0 GHz. It features 8.0 GB of system memory, providing 2.0 GB per core, and 75 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. In benchmark testing, the instance demonstrates top-tier single-core CPU performance and single-threaded decompression capabilities, though multi-core scaling and database operations show weaker relative performance. The shared CPU allocation offers a cost-efficient option for compute-focused workloads that benefit from modern instruction sets like AVX-512 without requiring dedicated hardware. This server is suitable for single-threaded processing, light web serving, and general-purpose utility tasks.
